description:
Analyze the spatial mismatch between service sector jobs and low-income housing availability across San Francisco zones using the datasets in /home/ga/urbansim_projects/data/sanfran_public.h5. Define low-income households as those with income at or below the citywide 25th percentile, and service jobs as those in sector 4 (Retail Trade) or 10 (Accommodation and Food Services). Calculate the mismatch ratio for each zone as service_jobs / (low_income_hhs + 1). Identify the top 30 zones with the highest mismatch ratios among significant employment centers (zones with at least 100 service jobs).

Document the analysis in a Jupyter notebook at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/notebooks/spatial_mismatch.ipynb using the UrbanSim (Python 3) kernel. Export the data for the top 30 zones (including zone_id, service_jobs, low_income_hhs, and mismatch_ratio) to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/worst_mismatch_zones.csv. Generate a scatter plot of service_jobs vs low_income_hhs for all significant zones, highlighting the top 30 worst mismatch zones, and save it to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/mismatch_scatter.png. All notebook cells should be executed with outputs visible.
